name: Kyla Jane Jamito
bio: Directed the overall research direction and coordinated the team throughout the project lifecycle. Spearheaded data collection, preprocessing, and analysis of bitter gourd leaf samples to build a reliable foundation for NPK deficiency detection.

name: Psalm Ashley Andal
bio: Focused on dataset curation, feature engineering, and model evaluation for the NPK classification pipeline. Contributed to statistical analysis and ensured data quality across all training and validation sets.

name: Chester Jefferson Andaya
bio: Bridged data science and software engineering — built and integrated both the frontend interface and backend services while also contributing to dataset preparation and model experimentation for the deficiency classifier.

name: John Michael Reyes
groupRole: Full Stack Developer, Data Scientist & ML Engineer
bio: Wore multiple hats across the stack — engineered the machine learning model, developed and deployed the prediction API, and contributed to both frontend and backend development. Core technical driver behind NPKnows.

name: Patrick Eva
bio: Worked on data annotation, exploratory analysis, and model validation for the NPK leaf classification system. Helped ensure the dataset accurately represented real-world deficiency conditions in bitter gourd crops.
